_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Pain in the upper abdomen that spreads into the back
Severe acute upper and abdominal pain
Three to four fold increase in the level of serum amylase within 48 hours of onset of pain

clinical_thresholds.note: within 48 hours of onset of pain
TMS pop-up scenario indicates checking for raised levels

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment
Serum Amylase, Lipase, LFT, CBC reports

claim.label: Post treatment Plain X ray erect Abdomen/USG/CT abdomen
Serum Amylase, Lipase, LFT, CBC reports

ppd_preauth.q: Are all the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Are the reports related Serum Amylase, Lipase, LFT, CBC test submitted?
Is the USG Abdomen report/CT Abdomen (Contrast) report submitted?
Does the report suggest raised plasma concentration of amylase and lipase?

cpd_claim.q: Is the detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and line of treatment submitted?
Are the post treatment Plain X ray erect Abdomen/USG/CT abdomen report submitted?
Are the reports related Serum Amylase, Lipase, LFT, CBC test submitted?
Is the discharge summary submitted with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Plasma concentration of amylase and lipase not reported as raised.
Clinical Notes missing evaluation findings, indications, or planned line of treatment.
Serum Amylase, Lipase, LFT, or CBC reports not submitted for pre-authorization.
USG Abdomen report or CT Abdomen (Contrast) not submitted for pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals or line of treatment not submitted for claim.
Discharge summary not submitted or lacks follow-up advice for claim.

clinical_key_pointers: The common causes include gall bladder stone disease, alcoholism and idiopathic where no obvious cause is discernible.

Further investigations need to be done to document severity of acute pancreatitis. These include CBC, BUN & serum creatinine, blood gas analysis, C-reactive protein. Chest X-ray PA and ultrasound to demonstrate pleural effusion.
